name: run_sql
description: Executes a SQL query against a registered database connection and returns the result set in the specified format. Supports SELECT, DML, and DDL statements subject to connection permissions.

query.description: The SQL query string to execute against the target database.
examples: SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE active = true LIMIT 50
UPDATE orders SET status = 'shipped' WHERE order_id = 12345
SELECT department, AVG(salary) as avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY department ORDER BY avg_salary DESC
query.$comment: Query is passed as a raw string; parameterization recommended for user-supplied values. Backend may enforce read-only mode per connection_id.

connection_id.description: The unique identifier of the pre-configured database connection to execute the query against.
connection_id.$comment: Connection credentials and DSN are stored server-side; client never sees raw credentials. Connection must be pre-registered in the platform.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow the query to run before cancelling it.
timeout_seconds.$comment: Long-running analytical queries may require higher values. Maximum of 300s prevents indefinite lock-holding.

max_rows.description: Maximum number of rows to return in the result set. Acts as a LIMIT safeguard.
max_rows.$comment: Default of 100 prevents accidental full-table dumps. For large data exports, use a dedicated ETL tool instead.

format.description: The output format for the query results.
format.$comment: 'table' is human-readable ASCII grid; 'json' is machine-readable array of objects; 'csv' is best for downstream file export.
